{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01NX4nPBxssaj2RyUJXZEiMs","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→\"use client\";\n     2→\n     3→import { useState } from \"react\";\n     4→import { useRouter } from \"next/navigation\";\n     5→import Link from \"next/link\";\n     6→\n     7→export default function LoginPage() {\n     8→  const [email, setEmail] = useState(\"\");\n     9→  const [password, setPassword] = useState(\"\");\n    10→  const [error, setError] = useState(\"\");\n    11→  const [loading, setLoading] = useState(false);\n    12→  const router = useRouter();\n    13→\n    14→  const handleSubmit = async (e: React.FormEvent) => {\n    15→    e.preventDefault();\n    16→    setError(\"\");\n    17→    setLoading(true);\n    18→\n    19→    try {\n    20→      const res = await fetch(\"/api/auth/login\", {\n    21→        method: \"POST\",\n    22→        headers: { \"Content-Type\": \"application/json\" },\n    23→        body: JSON.stringify({ email, password }),\n    24→      });\n    25→      const data = await res.json();\n    26→\n    27→      if (!res.ok) {\n    28→        setError(data.error || \"Login failed\");\n    29→        return;\n    30→      }\n    31→\n    32→      router.push(\"/\");\n    33→      router.refresh();\n    34→    } catch {\n    35→      setError(\"Something went wrong. Please try again.\");\n    36→    } finally {\n    37→      setLoading(false);\n    38→    }\n    39→  };\n    40→\n    41→  return (\n    42→    <div className=\"max-w-md mx-auto mt-4 sm:mt-12\">\n    43→      <div className=\"card\">\n    44→        <h1 className=\"text-2xl mb-1\">Welcome back</h1>\n    45→        <p\n    46→          className=\"mb-6\"\n    47→          style={{\n    48→            color: \"var(--color-text-secondary)\",\n    49→            fontFamily: \"system-ui, sans-serif\",\n    50→            fontSize: \"0.9rem\",\n    51→          }}\n    52→        >\n    53→          Sign in to continue reading with your groups\n    54→        </p>\n    55→\n    56→        {error && (\n    57→          <div\n    58→            className=\"mb-4 p-3 rounded-lg text-sm\"\n    59→            style={{\n    60→              backgroundColor: \"rgba(192, 57, 43, 0.08)\",\n    61→              color: \"var(--color-error)\",\n    62→              fontFamily: \"system-ui, sans-serif\",\n    63→            }}\n    64→            role=\"alert\"\n    65→            id=\"login-error\"\n    66→          >\n    67→            {error}\n    68→          </div>\n    69→        )}\n    70→\n    71→        <form onSubmit={handleSubmit} className=\"space-y-4\">\n    72→          <div>\n    73→            <label\n    74→              htmlFor=\"login-email\"\n    75→              className=\"block text-sm font-semibold mb-1\"\n    76→              style={{ fontFamily: \"system-ui, sans-serif\" }}\n    77→            >\n    78→              Email\n    79→            </label>\n    80→            <input\n    81→              id=\"login-email\"\n    82→              type=\"email\"\n    83→              className=\"input-field\"\n    84→              value={email}\n    85→              onChange={(e) => setEmail(e.target.value)}\n    86→              required\n    87→              placeholder=\"you@example.com\"\n    88→              autoComplete=\"email\"\n    89→              aria-describedby={error ? \"login-error\" : undefined}\n    90→            />\n    91→          </div>\n    92→\n    93→          <div>\n    94→            <label\n    95→              htmlFor=\"login-password\"\n    96→              className=\"block text-sm font-semibold mb-1\"\n    97→              style={{ fontFamily: \"system-ui, sans-serif\" }}\n    98→            >\n    99→              Password\n   100→            </label>\n   101→            <input\n   102→              id=\"login-password\"\n   103→              type=\"password\"\n   104→              className=\"input-field\"\n   105→              value={password}\n   106→              onChange={(e) => setPassword(e.target.value)}\n   107→              required\n   108→ {"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01ReLoCEBeUAwJyEv98es6XB","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→\"use client\";\n     2→\n     3→import { useState } from \"react\";\n     4→import { useRouter } from \"next/navigation\";\n     5→import Link from \"next/link\";\n     6→\n     7→export default function RegisterPage() {\n     8→  const [email, setEmail] = useState(\"\");\n     9→  const [password, setPassword] = useState(\"\");\n    10→  const [displayName, setDisplayName] = useState(\"\");\n    11→  const [error, setError] = useState(\"\");\n    12→  const [loading, setLoading] = useState(false);\n    13→  const router = useRouter();\n    14→\n    15→  const handleSubmit = async (e: React.FormEvent) => {\n    16→    e.preventDefault();\n    17→    setError(\"\");\n    18→    setLoading(true);\n    19→\n    20→    try {\n    21→      const res = await fetch(\"/api/auth/register\", {\n    22→        method: \"POST\",\n    23→        headers: { \"Content-Type\": \"application/json\" },\n    24→        body: JSON.stringify({ email, password, displayName }),\n    25→      });\n    26→      const data = await res.json();\n    27→\n    28→      if (!res.ok) {\n    29→        setError(data.error || \"Registration failed\");\n    30→        return;\n    31→      }\n    32→\n    33→      router.push(\"/\");\n    34→      router.refresh();\n    35→    } catch {\n    36→      setError(\"Something went wrong. Please try again.\");\n    37→    } finally {\n    38→      setLoading(false);\n    39→    }\n    40→  };\n    41→\n    42→  return (\n    43→    <div className=\"max-w-md mx-auto mt-4 sm:mt-12\">\n    44→      <div className=\"card\">\n    45→        <h1 className=\"text-2xl mb-1\">Create an account</h1>\n    46→        <p\n    47→          className=\"mb-6\"\n    48→          style={{\n    49→            color: \"var(--color-text-secondary)\",\n    50→            fontFamily: \"system-ui, sans-serif\",\n    51→            fontSize: \"0.9rem\",\n    52→          }}\n    53→        >\n    54→          Join Bookmate to find your reading companions\n    55→        </p>\n    56→\n    57→        {error && (\n    58→          <div\n    59→            className=\"mb-4 p-3 rounded-lg text-sm\"\n    60→            style={{\n    61→              backgroundColor: \"rgba(192, 57, 43, 0.08)\",\n    62→              color: \"var(--color-error)\",\n    63→              fontFamily: \"system-ui, sans-serif\",\n    64→            }}\n    65→            role=\"alert\"\n    66→            id=\"register-error\"\n    67→          >\n    68→            {error}\n    69→          </div>\n    70→        )}\n    71→\n    72→        <form onSubmit={handleSubmit} className=\"space-y-4\">\n    73→          <div>\n    74→            <label\n    75→              htmlFor=\"register-name\"\n    76→              className=\"block text-sm font-semibold mb-1\"\n    77→              style={{ fontFamily: \"system-ui, sans-serif\" }}\n    78→            >\n    79→              Display name\n    80→            </label>\n    81→            <input\n    82→              id=\"register-name\"\n    83→              type=\"text\"\n    84→              className=\"input-field\"\n    85→              value={displayName}\n    86→              onChange={(e) => setDisplayName(e.target.value)}\n    87→              required\n    88→              placeholder=\"How others will see you\"\n    89→              autoComplete=\"name\"\n    90→              aria-describedby={error ? \"register-error\" : undefined}\n    91→            />\n    92→          </div>\n    93→\n    94→          <div>\n    95→            <label\n    96→              htmlFor=\"register-email\"\n    97→              className=\"block text-sm font-semibold mb-1\"\n    98→              style={{ fontFamily: \"system-ui, sans-serif\" }}\n    99→            >\n   100→              Email\n   101→            </label>\n   102→            <input\n   103→              id=\"register-email\"\n   104→              type=\"email\"\n   105→              className=\"input-field\"\n   106→              value={email}\n   107→              onChange={(e) => setEmail(e.target.value)}\n   108→              required\n   109→              placeholder=\"you@example.com\"\n   110→              autoComplete=\"email\"\n   111→              aria-describedby={error ? \"register-error\" : undefined}\n   112→            />\n   113→          </div>\n   114→\n   115→          <div>\n   116→            <label\n   117→              htmlFor=\"register-password\"\n   118→              className=\"block text-sm font-semibold mb-1\"\n   119→              style={{ fontFamily: \"system-ui, sans-serif\" }}\n   120→            >\n   121→              Password\n   122→            </label>\n   123→            <input\n   124→              id=\"register-password\"\n   125→              type=\"password\"\n   126→              className=\"input-field\"\n   127→              value={password}\n   128→              onChange={(e) => setPassword(e.target.value)}\n   129→              required\n   130→              minLength={8}\n   131→              placeholder=\"At least 8 characters\"\n   132→              autoComplete=\"new-password\"\n   133→              aria-describedby={error ? \"register-error\" : undefined}\n   134→            />\n   135→          </div>\n   136→\n   137→          <button\n   138→            type=\"submit\"\n   139→            className=\"btn-primary w-full\"\n   140→            disabled={loading}\n   141→          >\n   142→            {loading ? \"Creating account...\" : \"Create account\"}\n   143→          </button>\n   144→        </form>\n   145→\n   146→        <p\n   147→          className=\"text-sm text-center mt-4\"\n   148→          style={{\n   149→ {"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01CF4zxUsi6YNoZEm2vAVR9s","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from \"next/server\";\n     2→import db from \"@/lib/db\";\n     3→import { requireAuth } from \"@/lib/session\";\n     4→import { createNotification } from \"@/lib/notifications\";\n     5→\n     6→/**\n     7→ * POST /api/ratings — Submit a rating for a group member\n     8→ *\n     9→ * Body: { listingId, ratedUserId, score (1-5), comment? }\n    10→ *\n    11→ * Rules:\n    12→ * - Both rater and rated must be members of the listing\n    13→ * - The listing must be full (group completed)\n    14→ * - Cannot rate yourself\n    15→ * - One rating per rater-rated pair per listing (can update)\n    16→ */\n    17→export async function POST(req: NextRequest) {\n    18→  const session = await requireAuth();\n    19→  if (!session) {\n    20→    return NextResponse.json(\n    21→      { error: \"Authentication required\" },\n    22→      { status: 401 }\n    23→    );\n    24→  }\n    25→\n    26→  const body = await req.json();\n    27→  const { listingId, ratedUserId, score, comment } = body;\n    28→\n    29→  if (listingId == null || ratedUserId == null || score == null) {\n    30→    return NextResponse.json(\n    31→      { error: \"listingId, ratedUserId, and score are required\" },\n    32→      { status: 400 }\n    33→    );\n    34→  }\n    35→\n    36→  // Validate listingId and ratedUserId are positive integers\n    37→  if (typeof listingId !== \"number\" || !Number.isInteger(listingId) || listingId < 1) {\n    38→    return NextResponse.json(\n    39→      { error: \"Invalid listingId\" },\n    40→      { status: 400 }\n    41→    );\n    42→  }\n    43→\n    44→  if (typeof ratedUserId !== \"number\" || !Number.isInteger(ratedUserId) || ratedUserId < 1) {\n    45→    return NextResponse.json(\n    46→      { error: \"Invalid ratedUserId\" },\n    47→      { status: 400 }\n    48→    );\n    49→  }\n    50→\n    51→  if (score < 1 || score > 5 || !Number.isInteger(score)) {\n    52→    return NextResponse.json(\n    53→      { error: \"Score must be an integer between 1 and 5\" },\n    54→      { status: 400 }\n    55→    );\n    56→  }\n    57→\n    58→  if (ratedUserId === session.userId) {\n    59→    return NextResponse.json(\n    60→      { error: \"You cannot rate yourself\" },\n    61→      { status: 400 }\n    62→    );\n    63→  }\n    64→\n    65→  if (comment && comment.length > 1000) {\n    66→    return NextResponse.json(\n    67→      { error: \"Comment must be at most 1000 characters\" },\n    68→      { status: 400 }\n    69→    );\n    70→  }\n    71→\n    72→  // Verify listing exists and is full\n    73→  const listing = db\n    74→    .prepare(\"SELECT id, book_title, is_full FROM listings WHERE id = ?\")\n    75→    .get(listingId) as { id: number; book_title: string; is_full: number } | undefined;\n    76→\n    77→  if (!listing) {\n    78→    return NextResponse.json(\n    79→      { error: \"Listing not found\" },\n    80→      { status: 404 }\n    81→    );\n    82→  }\n    83→\n    84→  if (!listing.is_full) {\n    85→    return NextResponse.json(\n    86→      { error: \"Ratings are only available for completed reading groups\" },\n    87→      { status: 400 }\n    88→    );\n    89→  }\n    90→\n    91→  // Verify both users are members\n    92→  const raterMember = db\n    93→    .prepare(\n    94→      \"SELECT 1 FROM listing_members WHERE listing_id = ? AND user_id = ?\"\n    95→    )\n    96→    .get(listingId, session.userId);\n    97→\n    98→  if (!raterMember) {\n    99→    return NextResponse.json(\n   100→      { error: \"You must be a member of this group to rate\" },\n   101→      { status: 403 }\n   102→    );\n   103→  }\n   104→\n   105→  const ratedMember = db\n   106→    .prepare(\n   107→      \"SELECT 1 FROM listing_members WHERE listing_id = ? AND user_id = ?\"\n   108→    )\n   109→    .get(listingId, ratedUserId);\n   110→\n   111→  if (!ratedMember) {\n   112→    return NextResponse.json(\n   113→      { error: \"The user you are rating must be a member of this group\" },\n   114→      { status: 400 }\n   115→    );\n   116→  }\n   117→\n   118→  // Upsert rating (update if exists)\n   119→  const existing = db\n   120→    .prepare(\n   121→      \"SELECT id FROM ratings WHERE listing_id = ? AND rater_id = ? AND rated_user_id = ?\"\n   122→    )\n   123→    .get(listingId, session.userId, ratedUserId) as { id: number } | undefined;\n   124→\n   125→  if (existing) {\n   126→    db.prepare(\n   127→      \"UPDATE ratings SET score = ?, comment = ?, created_at = datetime('now') WHERE id = ?\"\n   128→    ).run(score, comment || \"\", existing.id);\n   129→  } else {\n   130→    db.prepare(\n   131→      \"INSERT INTO ratings (listing_id, rater_id, rated_user_id, score, comment) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?)\"\n   132→    ).run(listingId, session.userId, ratedUserId, score, comment || \"\");\n   133→\n   134→    // Notify the rated user\n   135→    createNotification(\n   136→      ratedUserId,\n   137→      listingId,\n   138→      \"rating_received\",\n   139→      `Someone rated your participation in \"${listing.book_title}\"`\n   140→    );\n   141→  }\n   142→\n   143→  return NextResponse.json({ ok: true });\n   144→}\n   145→\n   146→/**\n   147→ * GET /api/ratings?listingId=X — Get ratings for a listing\n   148→ *\n   149→ * Returns ratings the current user has given and received in a listing,\n   150→ * plus the members who can still be rated.\n   151→ */\n   152→export async function GET(req: NextRequest) {\n   153→  const session = await requireAuth();\n   154→  if (!session) {\n   155→    return NextResponse.json(\n   156→      { error: \"Authentication required\" },\n   157→      { status: 401 }\n   158→    );\n   159→  }\n   160→\n   161→  const listingIdParam = req.nextUrl.searchParams.get(\"listingId\");\n   162→  if (!listingIdParam) {\n   163→    return NextResponse.json(\n   164→      { error: \"listingId parameter required\" },\n   165→      { status: 400 }\n   166→    );\n   167→  }\n   168→  const listingId = parseInt(listingIdParam, 10);\n   169→  if (isNaN(listingId)) {\n   170→    return NextResponse.json(\n   171→      { error: \"Invalid listingId\" },\n   172→      { status: 400 }\n   173→    );\n   174→  }\n   175→\n   176→  // Verify the current user is a member of this listing\n   177→  const isMember = db\n   178→    .prepare(\"SELECT 1 FROM listing_members WHERE listing_id = ? AND user_id = ?\")\n   179→    .get(listingId, session.userId);\n   180→  if (!isMember) {\n   181→    return NextResponse.json(\n   182→      { error: \"You are not a member of this group\" },\n   183→      { status: 403 }\n   184→    );\n   185→  }\n   186→\n   187→  // Ratings the current user has given in this listing\n   188→  const givenRatings = db\n   189→    .prepare(\n   190→      `SELECT r.rated_user_id, r.score, r.comment, u.display_name\n   191→       FROM ratings r\n   192→       JOIN users u ON r.rated_user_id = u.id\n   193→       WHERE r.listing_id = ? AND r.rater_id = ?`\n   194→    )\n   195→    .all(listingId, session.userId) as {\n   196→      rated_user_id: number;\n   197→      score: number;\n   198→      comment: string;\n   199→      display_name: string;\n   200→    }[];\n   201→\n   202→  // Ratings the current user has received in this listing\n   203→  const receivedRatings = db\n   204→    .prepare(\n   205→      `SELECT r.rater_id, r.score, r.comment, u.display_name\n   206→       FROM ratings r\n   207→       JOIN users u ON r.rater_id = u.id\n   208→       WHERE r.listing_id = ? AND r.rated_user_id = ?`\n   209→    )\n   210→    .all(listingId, session.userId) as {\n   211→      rater_id: number;\n   212→      score: number;\n   213→      comment: string;\n   214→      display_name: string;\n   215→    }[];\n   216→\n   217→  return NextResponse.json({\n   218→    givenRatings,\n   219→    receivedRatings,\n   220→  });\n   221→}\n   222→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_01AhP9Z1MGx66d2LahS9f6JA","session_id":"0f2f8ceb-12d1-4ef2-ada5-bdfef2314ecc","uuid":"9028abb6-d51a-474f-b812-e45ac5208a21","timestamp":"2026-03-23T21:01:53.015Z"}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_013WfujGmwNT3EXHKmziUJeB","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→import type { Metadata } from \"next\";\n     2→import db from \"@/lib/db\";\n     3→import ListingDetail from \"./ListingDetail\";\n     4→\n     5→interface ListingRow {\n     6→  book_title: string;\n     7→  book_author: string;\n     8→  book_cover_url: string;\n     9→  reading_pace: string;\n    10→  author_name: string;\n    11→  max_group_size: number;\n    12→}\n    13→\n    14→export async function generateMetadata({\n    15→  params,\n    16→}: {\n    17→  params: Promise<{ id: string }>;\n    18→}): Promise<Metadata> {\n    19→  const { id } = await params;\n    20→  const listingId = parseInt(id, 10);\n    21→\n    22→  if (isNaN(listingId)) {\n    23→    return { title: \"Listing not found — Bookmate\" };\n    24→  }\n    25→\n    26→  try {\n    27→    const listing = db\n    28→      .prepare(\n    29→        `SELECT l.book_title, l.book_author, l.book_cover_url, l.reading_pace,\n    30→                l.max_group_size, u.display_name as author_name\n    31→         FROM listings l\n    32→         JOIN users u ON l.author_id = u.id\n    33→         WHERE l.id = ?`\n    34→      )\n    35→      .get(listingId) as ListingRow | undefined;\n    36→\n    37→    if (!listing) {\n    38→      return { title: \"Listing not found — Bookmate\" };\n    39→    }\n    40→\n    41→    const title = `${listing.book_title} by ${listing.book_author} — Bookmate`;\n    42→    const description = `Join a reading group for \"${listing.book_title}\" by ${listing.book_author}. Pace: ${listing.reading_pace}. Up to ${listing.max_group_size} readers. Posted by ${listing.author_name}.`;\n    43→\n    44→    return {\n    45→      title,\n    46→      description,\n    47→      openGraph: {\n    48→        title,\n    49→        description,\n    50→        type: \"article\",\n    51→        ...(listing.book_cover_url\n    52→          ? {\n    53→              images: [\n    54→                {\n    55→                  url: listing.book_cover_url,\n    56→                  alt: `Cover of ${listing.book_title}`,\n    57→                },\n    58→              ],\n    59→            }\n    60→          : {}),\n    61→      },\n    62→      twitter: {\n    63→        card: listing.book_cover_url ? \"summary_large_image\" : \"summary\",\n    64→        title,\n    65→        description,\n    66→        ...(listing.book_cover_url ? { images: [listing.book_cover_url] } : {}),\n    67→      },\n    68→    };\n    69→  } catch {\n    70→    return { title: \"Listing — Bookmate\" };\n    71→  }\n    72→}\n    73→\n    74→export default function ListingPage() {\n    75→  return <ListingDetail />;\n    76→}\n    77→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_01AhP9Z1MGx66d2LahS9f6JA","session_id":"0f2f8ceb-12d1-4ef2-ada5-bdfef2314ecc","uuid":"21d95ecd-0e45-4910-91c9-6d07ba4dd3ad","timestamp":"2026-03-23T21:01:53.143Z"}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_018Cvg7TAv8wnrZG27q3WC5w","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from \"next/server\";\n     2→import db from \"@/lib/db\";\n     3→import { requireAuth } from \"@/lib/session\";\n     4→\n     5→/**\n     6→ * GET /api/profile/reputation?userId=X\n     7→ *\n     8→ * Returns a user's reputation score and rating breakdown.\n     9→ * If no userId is provided, returns the current user's reputation.\n    10→ */\n    11→export async function GET(req: NextRequest) {\n    12→  const session = await requireAuth();\n    13→  if (!session) {\n    14→    return NextResponse.json(\n    15→      { error: \"Authentication required\" },\n    16→      { status: 401 }\n    17→    );\n    18→  }\n    19→\n    20→  const userIdParam = req.nextUrl.searchParams.get(\"userId\");\n    21→  const userId = userIdParam ? parseInt(userIdParam, 10) : session.userId;\n    22→  const isOwnProfile = userId === session.userId;\n    23→\n    24→  if (userIdParam && isNaN(userId as number)) {\n    25→    return NextResponse.json(\n    26→      { error: \"Invalid userId parameter\" },\n    27→      { status: 400 }\n    28→    );\n    29→  }\n    30→\n    31→  // Average score\n    32→  const stats = db\n    33→    .prepare(\n    34→      `SELECT\n    35→        COUNT(*) as total_ratings,\n    36→        ROUND(AVG(score), 1) as average_score,\n    37→        SUM(CASE WHEN score = 5 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) as five_star,\n    38→        SUM(CASE WHEN score = 4 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) as four_star,\n    39→        SUM(CASE WHEN score = 3 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) as three_star,\n    40→        SUM(CASE WHEN score = 2 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) as two_star,\n    41→        SUM(CASE WHEN score = 1 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) as one_star\n    42→       FROM ratings\n    43→       WHERE rated_user_id = ?`\n    44→    )\n    45→    .get(userId) as {\n    46→      total_ratings: number;\n    47→      average_score: number | null;\n    48→      five_star: number;\n    49→      four_star: number;\n    50→      three_star: number;\n    51→      two_star: number;\n    52→      one_star: number;\n    53→    };\n    54→\n    55→  // Number of completed groups\n    56→  const completedGroups = db\n    57→    .prepare(\n    58→      `SELECT COUNT(*) as count\n    59→       FROM listing_members lm\n    60→       JOIN listings l ON lm.listing_id = l.id\n    61→       WHERE lm.user_id = ? AND l.is_full = 1`\n    62→    )\n    63→    .get(userId) as { count: number };\n    64→\n    65→  // Number of groups where this user has given ratings\n    66→  const groupsRated = db\n    67→    .prepare(\n    68→      `SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT listing_id) as count\n    69→       FROM ratings\n    70→       WHERE rater_id = ?`\n    71→    )\n    72→    .get(userId) as { count: number };\n    73→\n    74→  // Recent ratings received (with anonymized feedback)\n    75→  const recentRatings = db\n    76→    .prepare(\n    77→      `SELECT r.score, r.comment, r.created_at, l.book_title\n    78→       FROM ratings r\n    79→       JOIN listings l ON r.listing_id = l.id\n    80→       WHERE r.rated_user_id = ?\n    81→       ORDER BY r.created_at DESC\n    82→       LIMIT 10`\n    83→    )\n    84→    .all(userId) as {\n    85→      score: number;\n    86→      comment: string;\n    87→      created_at: string;\n    88→      book_title: string;\n    89→    }[];\n    90→\n    91→  // Strip comments from ratings when viewing other users' profiles (privacy)\n    92→  const sanitizedRatings = isOwnProfile\n    93→    ? recentRatings\n    94→    : recentRatings.map(({ score, created_at, book_title }) => ({\n    95→        score,\n    96→        created_at,\n    97→        book_title,\n    98→      }));\n    99→\n   100→  return NextResponse.json({\n   101→    reputation: {\n   102→      averageScore: stats.average_score || 0,\n   103→      totalRatings: stats.total_ratings,\n   104→      completedGroups: completedGroups.count,\n   105→      groupsRated: groupsRated.count,\n   106→      breakdown: {\n   107→        5: stats.five_star || 0,\n   108→        4: stats.four_star || 0,\n   109→        3: stats.three_star || 0,\n   110→        2: stats.two_star || 0,\n   111→        1: stats.one_star || 0,\n   112→      },\n   113→      recentRatings: sanitizedRatings,\n   114→    },\n   115→  });\n   116→}\n   117→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_01AhP9Z1MGx66d2LahS9f6JA","session_id":"0f2f8ceb-12d1-4ef2-ada5-bdfef2314ecc","uuid":"08c537fa-61b7-4723-b585-a3a7d282ddb5","timestamp":"2026-03-23T21:01:59.466Z"}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01JB1RWy8T9adNLN5t5amdjx","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from \"next/server\";\n     2→import bcrypt from \"bcryptjs\";\n     3→import db from \"@/lib/db\";\n     4→import { getSession } from \"@/lib/session\";\n     5→import { checkRateLimit } from \"@/lib/rate-limit\";\n     6→\n     7→export async function POST(req: NextRequest) {\n     8→  try {\n     9→    const { email, password, displayName } = await req.json();\n    10→\n    11→    if (!email || !password || !displayName) {\n    12→      return NextResponse.json(\n    13→        { error: \"Email, password, and display name are required\" },\n    14→        { status: 400 }\n    15→      );\n    16→    }\n    17→\n    18→    // Rate limit: 3 registrations per IP per 15 minutes\n    19→    const ip = req.headers.get(\"x-forwarded-for\")?.split(\",\")[0]?.trim() || \"unknown\";\n    20→    const { allowed, retryAfter } = checkRateLimit(`register:${ip}`, 3);\n    21→    if (!allowed) {\n    22→      return NextResponse.json(\n    23→        { error: \"Too many registration attempts. Please try again later.\" },\n    24→        { status: 429, headers: { \"Retry-After\": String(retryAfter) } }\n    25→      );\n    26→    }\n    27→\n    28→    if (password.length < 8) {\n    29→      return NextResponse.json(\n    30→        { error: \"Password must be at least 8 characters\" },\n    31→        { status: 400 }\n    32→      );\n    33→    }\n    34→\n    35→    // bcrypt only uses the first 72 bytes; cap to prevent DoS via slow hashing\n    36→    if (password.length > 72) {\n    37→      return NextResponse.json(\n    38→        { error: \"Password must be at most 72 characters\" },\n    39→        { status: 400 }\n    40→      );\n    41→    }\n    42→\n    43→    if (displayName.length > 100) {\n    44→      return NextResponse.json(\n    45→        { error: \"Display name must be at most 100 characters\" },\n    46→        { status: 400 }\n    47→      );\n    48→    }\n    49→\n    50→    if (email.length > 254) {\n    51→      return NextResponse.json(\n    52→        { error: \"Email address is too long\" },\n    53→        { status: 400 }\n    54→      );\n    55→    }\n    56→\n    57→    if (!/^[^\\s@]+@[^\\s@]+\\.[^\\s@]+$/.test(email.trim())) {\n    58→      return NextResponse.json(\n    59→        { error: \"Please provide a valid email address\" },\n    60→        { status: 400 }\n    61→      );\n    62→    }\n    63→\n    64→    // Normalize email before duplicate check (INSERT stores lowercase)\n    65→    const normalizedEmail = email.toLowerCase().trim();\n    66→\n    67→    // Check if email is already taken\n    68→    const existing = db\n    69→      .prepare(\"SELECT id FROM users WHERE email = ?\")\n    70→      .get(normalizedEmail);\n    71→    if (existing) {\n    72→      return NextResponse.json(\n    73→        { error: \"An account with this email already exists\" },\n    74→        { status: 409 }\n    75→      );\n    76→    }\n    77→\n    78→    const passwordHash = await bcrypt.hash(password, 12);\n    79→\n    80→    const result = db\n    81→      .prepare(\n    82→        \"INSERT INTO users (email, password_hash, display_name) VALUES (?, ?, ?)\"\n    83→      )\n    84→      .run(normalizedEmail, passwordHash, displayName.trim());\n    85→\n    86→    // Auto-login after registration\n    87→    const session = await getSession();\n    88→    session.userId = Number(result.lastInsertRowid);\n    89→    session.email = normalizedEmail;\n    90→    session.displayName = displayName.trim();\n    91→    await session.save();\n    92→\n    93→    return NextResponse.json(\n    94→      {\n    95→        user: {\n    96→          id: result.lastInsertRowid,\n    97→          email: normalizedEmail,\n    98→          displayName: displayName.trim(),\n    99→        },\n   100→      },\n   101→      { status: 201 }\n   102→    );\n   103→  } catch (error) {\n   104→    console.error(\"Registration error:\", error);\n   105→    return NextResponse.json(\n   106→      { error: \"Internal server error\" },\n   107→      { status: 500 }\n   108→    );\n   109→  }\n   110→}\n   111→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_01AhP9Z1MGx66d2LahS9f6JA","session_id":"0f2f8ceb-12d1-4ef2-ada5-bdfef2314ecc","uuid":"2fa0c77d-f0e2-4c73-83fd-d83a2ff87475","timestamp":"2026-03-23T21:02:02.146Z"}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_018qzCUksDjdjUtfqRoYYD7o","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→/**\n     2→ * Simple in-memory rate limiter for auth endpoints.\n     3→ * Uses a sliding window approach keyed by identifier (IP + route).\n     4→ * Not shared across cluster workers — acceptable for SQLite single-process setup.\n     5→ */\n     6→\n     7→interface RateLimitEntry {\n     8→  timestamps: number[];\n     9→}\n    10→\n    11→const store = new Map<string, RateLimitEntry>();\n    12→\n    13→// Clean up expired entries every 5 minutes\n    14→// Guard against multiple intervals on Next.js hot reload\n    15→const CLEANUP_INTERVAL = 5 * 60 * 1000;\n    16→const globalObj = globalThis as unknown as { __rateLimitCleanup?: ReturnType<typeof setInterval> };\n    17→if (!globalObj.__rateLimitCleanup) {\n    18→  globalObj.__rateLimitCleanup = setInterval(() => {\n    19→    const now = Date.now();\n    20→    for (const [key, entry] of store) {\n    21→      entry.timestamps = entry.timestamps.filter((t) => now - t < 15 * 60 * 1000);\n    22→      if (entry.timestamps.length === 0) {\n    23→        store.delete(key);\n    24→      }\n    25→    }\n    26→  }, CLEANUP_INTERVAL);\n    27→}\n    28→\n    29→/**\n    30→ * Check if a request should be rate limited.\n    31→ * @param key - Unique identifier (e.g., IP + email for login, IP for register)\n    32→ * @param maxAttempts - Maximum attempts allowed in the window\n    33→ * @param windowMs - Time window in milliseconds (default: 15 minutes)\n    34→ * @returns Object with allowed boolean and retryAfter seconds (0 if allowed)\n    35→ */\n    36→export function checkRateLimit(\n    37→  key: string,\n    38→  maxAttempts: number = 5,\n    39→  windowMs: number = 15 * 60 * 1000\n    40→): { allowed: boolean; retryAfter: number } {\n    41→  const now = Date.now();\n    42→  let entry = store.get(key);\n    43→\n    44→  if (!entry) {\n    45→    entry = { timestamps: [] };\n    46→    store.set(key, entry);\n    47→  }\n    48→\n    49→  // Remove expired timestamps\n    50→  entry.timestamps = entry.timestamps.filter((t) => now - t < windowMs);\n    51→\n    52→  if (entry.timestamps.length >= maxAttempts) {\n    53→    const oldestInWindow = entry.timestamps[0];\n    54→    const retryAfter = Math.ceil((oldestInWindow + windowMs - now) / 1000);\n    55→    return { allowed: false, retryAfter };\n    56→  }\n    57→\n    58→  entry.timestamps.push(now);\n    59→  return { allowed: true, retryAfter: 0 };\n    60→}\n    61→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_01AhP9Z1MGx66d2LahS9f6JA","session_id":"0f2f8ceb-12d1-4ef2-ada5-bdfef2314ecc","uuid":"507f6bd6-5967-4812-89c9-48b8ec0a72f5","timestamp":"2026-03-23T21:02:02.379Z"}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01KLxGCPGFroTUZqnJ3dfBUg","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from \"next/server\";\n     2→import db from \"@/lib/db\";\n     3→import { requireAuth } from \"@/lib/session\";\n     4→\n     5→export async function POST(\n     6→  req: NextRequest,\n     7→  { params }: { params: Promise<{ id: string }> }\n     8→) {\n     9→  const session = await requireAuth();\n    10→  if (!session) {\n    11→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Authentication required\" }, { status: 401 });\n    12→  }\n    13→\n    14→  const { id } = await params;\n    15→  const listingId = parseInt(id, 10);\n    16→  if (isNaN(listingId)) {\n    17→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Invalid listing ID\" }, { status: 400 });\n    18→  }\n    19→\n    20→  const listing = db\n    21→    .prepare(\"SELECT * FROM listings WHERE id = ?\")\n    22→    .get(listingId) as Record<string, unknown> | undefined;\n    23→\n    24→  if (!listing) {\n    25→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Listing not found\" }, { status: 404 });\n    26→  }\n    27→\n    28→  if (listing.author_id !== session.userId) {\n    29→    return NextResponse.json(\n    30→      { error: \"Only the listing author can set the Telegram link\" },\n    31→      { status: 403 }\n    32→    );\n    33→  }\n    34→\n    35→  const { telegramLink } = await req.json();\n    36→\n    37→  if (!telegramLink || !telegramLink.startsWith(\"https://t.me/\") || telegramLink.length > 512) {\n    38→    return NextResponse.json(\n    39→      { error: \"Please provide a valid Telegram invite link (https://t.me/..., max 512 characters)\" },\n    40→      { status: 400 }\n    41→    );\n    42→  }\n    43→\n    44→  db.prepare(\"UPDATE listings SET telegram_link = ? WHERE id = ?\").run(\n    45→    telegramLink.trim(),\n    46→    listingId\n    47→  );\n    48→\n    49→  return NextResponse.json({ ok: true });\n    50→}\n    51→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_01AhP9Z1MGx66d2LahS9f6JA","session_id":"0f2f8ceb-12d1-4ef2-ada5-bdfef2314ecc","uuid":"26e13990-21ee-4ea5-8f1b-b5d296de4833","timestamp":"2026-03-23T21:02:06.059Z"}

  2→import db from \"@/lib/db\";\n     3→import { requireAuth } from \"@/lib/session\";\n     4→\n     5→export async function POST(\n     6→  req: NextRequest,\n     7→  { params }: { params: Promise<{ id: string }> }\n     8→) {\n     9→  const session = await requireAuth();\n    10→  if (!session) {\n    11→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Authentication required\" }, { status: 401 });\n    12→  }\n    13→\n    14→  const { id } = await params;\n    15→  const listingId = parseInt(id, 10);\n    16→  if (isNaN(listingId)) {\n    17→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Invalid listing ID\" }, { status: 400 });\n    18→  }\n    19→\n    20→  const listing = db\n    21→    .prepare(\"SELECT * FROM listings WHERE id = ?\")\n    22→    .get(listingId) as Record<string, unknown> | undefined;\n    23→\n    24→  if (!listing) {\n    25→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Listing not found\" }, { status: 404 });\n    26→  }\n    27→\n    28→  if (listing.author_id !== session.userId) {\n    29→    return NextResponse.json(\n    30→      { error: \"Only the listing author can set the Discord link\" },\n    31→      { status: 403 }\n    32→    );\n    33→  }\n    34→\n    35→  const { discordLink } = await req.json();\n    36→\n    37→  if (\n    38→    !discordLink ||\n    39→    discordLink.length > 512 ||\n    40→    !(\n    41→      discordLink.startsWith(\"https://discord.gg/\") ||\n    42→      discordLink.startsWith(\"https://discord.com/invite/\")\n    43→    )\n    44→  ) {\n    45→    return NextResponse.json(\n    46→      {\n    47→        error:\n    48→          \"Please provide a valid Discord invite link (https://discord.gg/... or https://discord.com/invite/..., max 512 characters)\",\n    49→      },\n    50→      { status: 400 }\n    51→    );\n    52→  }\n    53→\n    54→  db.prepare(\"UPDATE listings SET discord_link = ? WHERE id = ?\").run(\n    55→    discordLink.trim(),\n    56→    listingId\n    57→  );\n    58→\n    59→  return NextResponse.json({ ok: true });\n    60→}\n    61→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_01AhP9Z1MGx66d2LahS9f6JA","session_id":"0f2f8ceb-12d1-4ef2-ada5-bdfef2314ecc","uuid":"38d23cb4-4c38-48c1-9410-19e3150623ce","timestamp":"2026-03-23T21:02:06.527Z"}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01HXZDTT4WMm6jSprLVMWkYi","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→\"use client\";\n     2→\n     3→import { useEffect, useState, useCallback, useRef } from \"react\";\n     4→import Link from \"next/link\";\n     5→import BookCover from \"@/components/BookCover\";\n     6→\n     7→interface Listing {\n     8→  id: number;\n     9→  book_title: string;\n    10→  book_author: string;\n    11→  book_cover_url: string;\n    12→  language: string;\n    13→  reading_pace: string;\n    14→  start_date: string;\n    15→  meeting_format: string;\n    16→  max_group_size: number;\n    17→  requires_approval: number;\n    18→  platform_preference: string;\n    19→  member_count: number;\n    20→  author_name: string;\n    21→  created_at: string;\n    22→}\n    23→\n    24→export default function HomePage() {\n    25→  const [listings, setListings] = useState<Listing[]>([]);\n    26→  const [loading, setLoading] = useState(true);\n    27→  const [error, setError] = useState<string | null>(null);\n    28→\n    29→  // Search & filter state\n    30→  const [searchQuery, setSearchQuery] = useState(\"\");\n    31→  const [meetingFormat, setMeetingFormat] = useState(\"\");\n    32→  const [readingPace, setReadingPace] = useState(\"\");\n    33→  const [startDateFrom, setStartDateFrom] = useState(\"\");\n    34→  const [sort, setSort] = useState(\"newest\");\n    35→  const [filtersOpen, setFiltersOpen] = useState(false);\n    36→\n    37→  // Pagination state\n    38→  const [page, setPage] = useState(1);\n    39→  const [totalPages, setTotalPages] = useState(1);\n    40→  const [totalResults, setTotalResults] = useState(0);\n    41→\n    42→  const debounceRef = useRef<ReturnType<typeof setTimeout>>(undefined);\n    43→\n    44→  const fetchListings = useCallback(\n    45→    (query: string, format: string, sortBy: string, pace?: string, dateFrom?: string, pageNum?: number) => {\n    46→      setLoading(true);\n    47→      const params = new URLSearchParams();\n    48→      if (query) params.set(\"q\", query);\n    49→      if (format) params.set(\"meeting_format\", format);\n    50→      if (pace) params.set(\"reading_pace\", pace);\n    51→      if (dateFrom) params.set(\"start_date_from\", dateFrom);\n    52→      if (sortBy) params.set(\"sort\", sortBy);\n    53→      if (pageNum && pageNum > 1) params.set(\"page\", String(pageNum));\n    54→\n    55→      const qs = params.toString();\n    56→      fetch(`/api/listings${qs ? `?${qs}` : \"\"}`)\n    57→        .then((r) => {\n    58→          if (!r.ok) throw new Error(\"Failed to load listings\");\n    59→          return r.json();\n    60→        })\n    61→        .then((data) => {\n    62→          setListings(data.listings || []);\n    63→          if (data.pagination) {\n    64→            setTotalPages(data.pagination.totalPages || 1);\n    65→            setTotalResults(data.pagination.total || 0);\n    66→          }\n    67→          setError(null);\n    68→          setLoading(false);\n    69→        })\n    70→        .catch((err) => {\n    71→          setError(err.message || \"Could not load listings. Please try again.\");\n    72→          setLoading(false);\n    73→        });\n    74→    },\n    75→    []\n    76→  );\n    77→\n    78→  // Initial fetch\n    79→  useEffect(() => {\n    80→    fetchListings(\"\", \"\", \"newest\", \"\", \"\", 1);\n    81→  }, [fetchListings]);\n    82→\n    83→  // Debounced search\n    84→  const handleSearchChange = (value: string) => {\n    85→    setSearchQuery(value);\n    86→    setPage(1);\n    87→    if (debounceRef.current) clearTimeout(debounceRef.current);\n    88→    debounceRef.current = setTimeout(() => {\n    89→      fetchListings(value, meetingFormat, sort, readingPace, startDateFrom, 1);\n    90→    }, 400);\n    91→  };\n    92→\n    93→  // Immediate filter changes\n    94→  const handleFormatChange = (value: string) => {\n    95→    setMeetingFormat(value);\n    96→    setPage(1);\n    97→    fetchListings(searchQuery, value, sort, readingPace, startDateFrom, 1);\n    98→  };\n    99→\n   100→  const handlePaceChange = (value: string) => {"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_01AhP9Z1MGx66d2LahS9f6JA","session_id":"0f2f8ceb-12d1-4ef2-ada5-bdfef2314ecc","uuid":"4fbff31d-f103-444a-a6fb-f61aa2c06f6a","timestamp":"2026-03-23T21:02:09.826Z"}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01RZaCdLkLgCaBwpVuFi1eoo","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→\"use client\";\n     2→\n     3→import Link from \"next/link\";\n     4→import { useEffect, useState, useRef } from \"react\";\n     5→import { useRouter, usePathname } from \"next/navigation\";\n     6→\n     7→interface User {\n     8→  id: number;\n     9→  email: string;\n    10→  displayName: string;\n    11→}\n    12→\n    13→export default function Navbar() {\n    14→  const [user, setUser] = useState<User | null>(null);\n    15→  const [notifCount, setNotifCount] = useState(0);\n    16→  const [menuOpen, setMenuOpen] = useState(false);\n    17→  const [mobileMenuOpen, setMobileMenuOpen] = useState(false);\n    18→  const router = useRouter();\n    19→  const pathname = usePathname();\n    20→  const menuRef = useRef<HTMLDivElement>(null);\n    21→\n    22→  useEffect(() => {\n    23→    fetch(\"/api/auth/me\")\n    24→      .then((r) => r.json())\n    25→      .then((data) => {\n    26→        if (data.user) {\n    27→          setUser(data.user);\n    28→          setNotifCount(data.user.unreadNotificationCount || 0);\n    29→        }\n    30→      })\n    31→      .catch(() => {\n    32→        // Auth check failed — stay logged out\n    33→      });\n    34→  }, []);\n    35→\n    36→  // Close menus on outside click\n    37→  useEffect(() => {\n    38→    const handleClickOutside = (e: MouseEvent) => {\n    39→      if (menuRef.current && !menuRef.current.contains(e.target as Node)) {\n    40→        setMenuOpen(false);\n    41→      }\n    42→    };\n    43→    document.addEventListener(\"mousedown\", handleClickOutside);\n    44→    return () => document.removeEventListener(\"mousedown\", handleClickOutside);\n    45→  }, []);\n    46→\n    47→  // Close mobile menu on route change\n    48→  useEffect(() => {\n    49→    setMobileMenuOpen(false);\n    50→    setMenuOpen(false);\n    51→  }, [pathname]);\n    52→\n    53→  const handleLogout = async () => {\n    54→    await fetch(\"/api/auth/logout\", { method: \"POST\" });\n    55→    setUser(null);\n    56→    setMenuOpen(false);\n    57→    setMobileMenuOpen(false);\n    58→    router.push(\"/\");\n    59→    router.refresh();\n    60→  };\n    61→\n    62→  return (\n    63→    <nav\n    64→      className=\"border-b sticky top-0 z-50\"\n    65→      style={{\n    66→        borderColor: \"var(--color-border)\",\n    67→        backgroundColor: \"var(--color-bg)\",\n    68→      }}\n    69→    >\n    70→      <div className=\"max-w-5xl mx-auto px-3 sm:px-4 h-14 sm:h-16 flex items-center justify-between\">\n    71→        <Link\n    72→          href=\"/\"\n    73→          className=\"text-lg sm:text-xl font-bold\"\n    74→          style={{ color: \"var(--color-accent)\", letterSpacing: \"-0.03em\" }}\n    75→          onClick={() => setMobileMenuOpen(false)}\n    76→        >\n    77→          bookmate\n    78→        </Link>\n    79→\n    80→        {/* Desktop nav */}"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_01AhP9Z1MGx66d2LahS9f6JA","session_id":"0f2f8ceb-12d1-4ef2-ada5-bdfef2314ecc","uuid":"301b1b99-6ca6-4c4e-967c-28ce10a9ec9b","timestamp":"2026-03-23T21:02:10.692Z"}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01FRNcsvd6MtekKPUwY9eRBu","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from \"next/server\";\n     2→import db from \"@/lib/db\";\n     3→import { requireAuth } from \"@/lib/session\";\n     4→\n     5→const AVAILABLE_GENRES = [\n     6→  \"Literary Fiction\",\n     7→  \"Science Fiction\",\n     8→  \"Fantasy\",\n     9→  \"Mystery\",\n    10→  \"Thriller\",\n    11→  \"Romance\",\n    12→  \"Historical Fiction\",\n    13→  \"Horror\",\n    14→  \"Dystopian\",\n    15→  \"Adventure\",\n    16→  \"Contemporary Fiction\",\n    17→  \"Magical Realism\",\n    18→  \"Classics\",\n    19→  \"Short Stories\",\n    20→  \"Young Adult\",\n    21→  \"Graphic Novels\",\n    22→];\n    23→\n    24→interface GenreRow {\n    25→  genre: string;\n    26→}\n    27→\n    28→export async function GET() {\n    29→  const session = await requireAuth();\n    30→  if (!session) {\n    31→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Authentication required\" }, { status: 401 });\n    32→  }\n    33→\n    34→  const userGenres = db.prepare(\n    35→    \"SELECT genre FROM user_genres WHERE user_id = ? ORDER BY genre\"\n    36→  ).all(session.userId) as GenreRow[];\n    37→\n    38→  return NextResponse.json({\n    39→    genres: userGenres.map((g) => g.genre),\n    40→    available: AVAILABLE_GENRES,\n    41→  });\n    42→}\n    43→\n    44→export async function PUT(req: NextRequest) {\n    45→  const session = await requireAuth();\n    46→  if (!session) {\n    47→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Authentication required\" }, { status: 401 });\n    48→  }\n    49→\n    50→  const { genres } = await req.json();\n    51→\n    52→  if (!Array.isArray(genres)) {\n    53→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"genres must be an array\" }, { status: 400 });\n    54→  }\n    55→\n    56→  // Validate all genres\n    57→  const validGenres = genres.filter((g: string) => AVAILABLE_GENRES.includes(g));\n    58→\n    59→  // Replace all genres in a transaction\n    60→  const updateGenres = db.transaction(() => {\n    61→    db.prepare(\"DELETE FROM user_genres WHERE user_id = ?\").run(session.userId);\n    62→    const insert = db.prepare(\"INSERT INTO user_genres (user_id, genre) VALUES (?, ?)\");\n    63→    for (const genre of validGenres) {\n    64→      insert.run(session.userId, genre);\n    65→    }\n    66→  });\n    67→\n    68→  updateGenres();\n    69→\n    70→  return NextResponse.json({ genres: validGenres });\n    71→}\n    72→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_01AhP9Z1MGx66d2LahS9f6JA","session_id":"0f2f8ceb-12d1-4ef2-ada5-bdfef2314ecc","uuid":"aa1b437b-7860-4188-a413-fc8a4e2ae6e8","timestamp":"2026-03-23T21:02:13.124Z"}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01RsaKdLuxD3Fhz7SYkJ56oT","type":"tool_result","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Now I have a comprehensive understanding. Let me provide my findings to the user:\n\n---\n\n## Investigation Summary\n\nBased on my thorough analysis of the Bookmate codebase, here are my findings regarding the \"Failed to find Server Action 'x'\" errors appearing in PM2 logs:\n\n### 1. **No Server Actions Are Defined in the App**\n\nThe Bookmate application **does not use Next.js Server Actions at all**. Evidence:\n\n- **Zero `'use server'` directives**: Grep search returned no results across the entire codebase\n- **Empty server reference manifest**: `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/.next/server/server-reference-manifest.json` contains only:\n  ```json\n  {\"node\":{},\"edge\":{},\"encryptionKey\":\"...\"}\n  ```\n  This manifest would contain all Server Action definitions if any existed.\n- **Traditional API routes only**: All form submissions use `fetch()` to call `/api/*` endpoints (e.g., `/api/auth/login`, `/api/listings/create`)\n\n### 2. **Architecture Confirmation**\n\nThe app uses a standard Next.js App Router pattern:\n- Client pages use `\"use client\"` with `fetch()` calls to API routes\n- All database operations go through `/api/*` route handlers\n- No form actions or Server Action calls anywhere in the codebase\n\n**Example from `/app/auth/login/page.tsx`:**\n```typescript\nconst res = await fetch(\"/api/auth/login\", {\n  method: \"POST\",\n  headers: { \"Content-Type\": \"application/json\" },\n  body: JSON.stringify({ email, password }),\n});\n```\n\n### 3. **Source of the \"x\" Requests: External Probing**\n\nThe action ID being literally `\"x\"` is **highly suspicious and indicates external probing** rather than a legitimate client-side bug:\n\n**Why it's definitely external:**\n- Single character \"x\" is not a valid/meaningful action ID\n- Would never be generated by legitimate Next.js code\n- Appears in logs starting from 2026-03-20 with consistent frequency\n- A client-side bug would reference a real action ID or fail silently\n\n**Likely sources:**\n- Security scanners or penetration testers\n- Bots probing for Server Action endpoints\n- Browser extensions making random requests\n- Generic HTTP scanners testing `/api/*` routes\n\n### 4. **No Legitimate Client-Side Bug**\n\nSince your codebase has:\n- Zero Server Actions defined\n- No `useFormAction` hooks\n- No references to action IDs anywhere\n- Only standard `fetch()` API calls\n\nThere is **nothing to fix** in your code. These are 100% external requests to non-existent Server Action endpoints.\n\n### Recommendation\n\nThe errors are harmless but noisy. Consider adding middleware to:\n1. Silently ignore or rate-limit requests to non-existent Server Action endpoints\n2. Block obviously malicious probes early\n3. Or simply ignore these errors as they don't affect functionality\n\nThe app is functioning correctly."},{"type":"text","text":"agentId: a1a661b11c617a984 (use SendMessage with to: 'a1a661b11c617a984' to continue this agent)\n<usage>total_tokens: 54315\ntool_uses: 46\nduration_ms: 62033</usage>"}]}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"0f2f8ceb-12d1-4ef2-ada5-bdfef2314ecc","uuid":"88453200-fb28-4ae6-b566-ed0e8338156e","timestamp":"2026-03-23T21:02:14.037Z","tool_use_result":{"status":"completed","prompt":"Quick investigation: The PM2 error logs show these errors:\n```\n2026-03-23 20:47:53 +00:00: [Error: Failed to find Server Action \"x\". This request might be from an older or newer deployment.\n2026-03-23 20:53:21 +00:00: [Error: Failed to find Server Action \"x\". This request might be from an older or newer deployment.\n2026-03-23 20:56:56 +00:00: [Error: Failed to find Server Action \"x\". This request might be from an older or newer deployment.\n```\n\nThe action ID is literally \"x\" which seems suspicious — could be someone probing the server or a bot.\n\nCheck the app at /root/Projects/bookmate/app/ for:\n1. Are there any Server Actions (use server) defined in the codebase?\n2. Is this just external probing, or is there a legitimate client-side bug?\n3. Look at any form actions or useFormAction hooks that might send incorrect action IDs","agentId":"a1a661b11c617a984","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Now I have a comprehensive understanding. `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/.next/server/server-reference-manifest.json` contains only:\n  ```json\n  {\"node\":{},\"edge\":{},\"encryptionKey\":\"...\"}\n  ```\n  This manifest would contain all Server Action definitions if any existed.\n- **Traditional API routes only**: All form submissions use `fetch()` to call `/api/*` endpoints (e.g., `/api/auth/login`, `/api/listings/create`)\n\n### 2. **Architecture Confirmation**\n\nThe app uses a standard Next.js App Router pattern:\n- Client pages use `\"use client\"` with `fetch()` calls to API routes\n- All database operations go through `/api/*` route handlers\n- No form actions or Server Action calls anywhere in the codebase\n\n**Example from `/app/auth/login/page.tsx`:**\n```typescript\nconst res = await fetch(\"/api/auth/login\", Probing**\n\nThe action ID being literally `\"x\"` is **highly suspicious and indicates external probing** rather than a legitimate client-side bug:\n\n**Why it's definitely external:**\n- Single character \"x\" is not a valid/meaningful action ID\n- Would never be generated by legitimate Next.js code\n- Appears in logs starting from 2026-03-20 with consistent frequency\n- A client-side bug would reference a real action ID or fail silently\n\n**Likely sources:**\n- Security scanners or penetration testers\n- Bots probing for Server Action endpoints\n- Browser extensions making random requests\n- Generic HTTP scanners testing `/api/*` routes\n\n### 4. **No Legitimate Client-Side Bug**\n\nSince your codebase has:\n- Zero Server Actions defined\n- No `useFormAction` hooks\n- No Silently ignore or rate-limit requests to non-existent Server Action endpoints\n2. Block obviously malicious probes early\n3. {"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01MRn6LCNAFL3asV45uxvFie","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from \"next/server\";\n     2→import db from \"@/lib/db\";\n     3→import { requireAuth } from \"@/lib/session\";\n     4→import { isBotConfigured, generateGroupDeepLink } from \"@/lib/telegram\";\n     5→\n     6→/**\n     7→ * GET /api/listings/[id]/auto-telegram\n     8→ *\n     9→ * Returns the Telegram deep link for one-click group creation.\n    10→ * Only available to the listing author when the group is full\n    11→ * and no Telegram link has been set yet.\n    12→ */\n    13→export async function GET(\n    14→  _req: NextRequest,\n    15→  { params }: { params: Promise<{ id: string }> }\n    16→) {\n    17→  const session = await requireAuth();\n    18→  if (!session) {\n    19→    return NextResponse.json(\n    20→      { error: \"Authentication required\" },\n    21→      { status: 401 }\n    22→    );\n    23→  }\n    24→\n    25→  if (!isBotConfigured()) {\n    26→    return NextResponse.json(\n    27→      { error: \"Telegram bot not configured\", botConfigured: false },\n    28→      { status: 404 }\n    29→    );\n    30→  }\n    31→\n    32→  const { id } = await params;\n    33→  const listingId = parseInt(id, 10);\n    34→  if (isNaN(listingId)) {\n    35→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Invalid listing ID\" }, { status: 400 });\n    36→  }\n    37→\n    38→  const listing = db\n    39→    .prepare(\"SELECT * FROM listings WHERE id = ?\")\n    40→    .get(listingId) as Record<string, unknown> | undefined;\n    41→\n    42→  if (!listing) {\n    43→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Listing not found\" }, { status: 404 });\n    44→  }\n    45→\n    46→  if (listing.author_id !== session.userId) {\n    47→    return NextResponse.json(\n    48→      { error: \"Only the organizer can create the Telegram group\" },\n    49→      { status: 403 }\n    50→    );\n    51→  }\n    52→\n    53→  if (!listing.is_full) {\n    54→    return NextResponse.json(\n    55→      { error: \"Group must be full before creating a Telegram group\" },\n    56→      { status: 400 }\n    57→    );\n    58→  }\n    59→\n    60→  if (listing.telegram_link) {\n    61→    return NextResponse.json(\n    62→      { error: \"Telegram link already set\", telegramLink: listing.telegram_link },\n    63→      { status: 400 }\n    64→    );\n    65→  }\n    66→\n    67→  const deepLink = await generateGroupDeepLink(listingId);\n    68→  if (!deepLink) {\n    69→    return NextResponse.json(\n    70→      { error: \"Failed to generate deep link. Check bot configuration.\" },\n    71→      { status: 500 }\n    72→    );\n    73→  }\n    74→\n    75→  return NextResponse.json({\n    76→    deepLink,\n    77→    instructions:\n    78→      \"Click the link to open Telegram and create a group. The bot will automatically generate an invite link for your reading group.\",\n    79→  });\n    80→}\n    81→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_01AhP9Z1MGx66d2LahS9f6JA","session_id":"0f2f8ceb-12d1-4ef2-ada5-bdfef2314ecc","uuid":"305f4928-a77f-4287-8427-4f46af7e55d0","timestamp":"2026-03-23T21:02:23.899Z"}
{"type":"system","subtype":"task_notification","task_id":"a0d37eb2ee7074b40","tool_use_id":"toolu_01AhP9Z1MGx66d2LahS9f6JA","status":"completed","output_file":"","summary":"Code review round 5","usage":{"total_tokens":89628,"tool_uses":43,"duration_ms":138983},"uuid":"9f70bc21-dc36-469f-a05e-1e065c6f7bd6","session_id":"0f2f8ceb-12d1-4ef2-ada5-bdfef2314ecc"}
{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01AhP9Z1MGx66d2LahS9f6JA","type":"tool_result","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Now I have a thorough understanding of the codebase. Let me compile the findings.\n\n---\n\nReviewing the Bookmate application. Here are the high-confidence issues found across the specified areas.\n\n---\n\n## Critical Issues\n\n### 1. `fetchListing` is not stable — called inside `useEffect` without being in deps array, and recreated on every render\n\n**File:** `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/listings/[id]/ListingDetail.tsx`, lines 86–109\n\n`fetchListing` is defined as a plain `async function` inside the component body (not wrapped in `useCallback`), so it is recreated on every render. The `useEffect` at line 106–109 explicitly suppresses the ESLint exhaustive-deps warning with a comment on line 108. The function closes over `id` from `useParams()`, which is correct, but the suppression comment hides the underlying design issue.\n\nThe real bug here is more subtle: when `fetchListing` is called after `handleJoin`, `handleApply`, `handleDecision`, `handleTelegramSave`, and `handleDiscordSave` (lines 207, 229, 251, 282, 331), there is no guard against a component that has been unmounted between the time the API call finished and when `setListing` / `setLoading` is called. In Next.js App Router this is less likely to cause crashes, but it can produce a state update on an unmounted component in edge cases, particularly if the user navigates away during the loading state.\n\nMore immediately, `fetchListing` itself never sets `loading` back to `true` before refetching (only the initial call starts with `loading = true`), so refreshes after join/apply/decision happen silently with no loading indicator but also with no error recovery if the re-fetch fails.\n\n**Confidence: 80** — The missing `loading = true` on re-fetches is a real UX bug where errors after a join/apply are silent at the loading level (though `setFetchError(true)` is called).\n\n---\n\n### 2. `notifyGroupFull` notifies author who may already be a member — double notification on join\n\n**File:** `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/lib/notifications.ts`, lines 113–139  \n**Related:** `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/listings/[id]/join/route.ts`, lines 98–103\n\nIn `notifyGroupFull`, all rows in `listing_members` are fetched and notified with the \"group full\" message (line 119). The author is auto-joined as a member at listing creation (`create/route.ts` line 117–119). When another user fills the group, `notifyListingAuthor` is called (line 99 of join route) AND then `notifyGroupFull` is called (line 102), which again includes the author in the members list. The author therefore receives two separate notifications: one for the new member joining, and a second \"group is now full\" one. This is likely unintentional — the author is spammed.\n\n**Confidence: 85** — The author is always in `listing_members`, so `notifyGroupFull` always includes them. They will consistently receive both a \"new_member\" and \"group_full\" notification for the same join event.\n\n---\n\n### 3. `telegram/route.ts` does not validate the listing is full before accepting the link\n\n**File:** `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/listings/[id]/telegram/route.ts`, lines 37–47  \n**File:** `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/listings/[id]/discord/route.ts`, lines 37–56\n\nThe Telegram and Discord link-setting routes validate the format and author authorization, but do not check `listing.is_full`. This means the author can store a Telegram or Discord invite link at any time — even before the group is full. The listing detail page only shows these links to members when `is_full` is set, but the data is written to the database prematurely. This bypasses the intended flow and also means the `DELETE`/`PATCH` lock (which prevents editing once a platform link is shared) triggers too early if the author sets a link pre-fill.\n\n**Confidence: 85** — The API allows writing platform links to a non-full listing; the UI doesn't expose the input until full, but the API does not enforce this constraint.\n\n---\n\n### 4. XSS risk: `listing.book_title` rendered unsanitized in `book_title` channel name display\n\n**File:** `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/listings/[id]/ListingDetail.tsx`, line 1075\n\n```tsx\n<strong>#{listing.book_title.toLowerCase().replace(/[^a-z0-9]+/g, \"-\").slice(0, 30)}</strong>\n```\n\n`listing.book_title` here comes directly from the API response, which sources it from the database. The database stores whatever `bookTitle` was submitted by the listing creator (validated to max 300 chars but not otherwise sanitized). While React's JSX rendering of `{text}` does protect against XSS in text nodes, the concern here is when `book_title` is used in `generateMetadata` (line 41–42 of `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/listings/[id]/page.tsx`):\n\n```ts\nconst description = `Join a reading group for \"${listing.book_title}\" by ${listing.book_author}. ...Posted by ${listing.author_name}.`;\n```\n\nThese strings from the database are injected directly into the Open Graph `description` metadata field without HTML escaping. In the meta tag context Next.js handles escaping, so this is low risk. However, note that `book_title` and `book_author` also come from the Open Library API (user-selected, but passed through `bookTitle.trim()` to the DB). The risk is present but mitigated by React's escaping in JSX rendering. **Confidence drops to borderline** — dropping this finding.\n\n---\n\n### 5. Race condition: `listing.is_full` checked before the transaction in `apply/route.ts`\n\n**File:** `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/listings/[id]/apply/route.ts`, lines 43–45\n\nThe initial `is_full` check at line 43 is outside the transaction. However, the transaction at line 58–95 re-checks `is_full` at line 59–65. This is correctly done — the outer check is a fast-path optimization, not the authoritative check. The transaction re-checks authoritatively. This is fine design. No issue here.\n\n---\n\n### 6. `applicationId` is not validated as a number in `applications/PATCH`\n\n**File:** `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/listings/[id]/applications/route.ts`, lines 33–40\n\n```ts\nconst { applicationId, action } = body;\n\nif (!applicationId || ![\"approve\", \"reject\"].includes(action)) {\n```\n\n`applicationId` is only checked for truthiness (`!applicationId`), not validated as a positive integer. If a client sends `applicationId: \"1; DROP TABLE listing_applications\"` it would be passed to the prepared statement, which is safe (SQLite parameterized queries prevent SQL injection). However, if `applicationId` is `0`, `false`, or `\"\"` the check correctly rejects it. If it is a non-integer string like `\"abc\"`, better-sqlite3 will coerce it and the `SELECT ... WHERE id = ?` will simply return no rows (404). This is not a real bug in practice given prepared statements, but the validation gap is real.\n\n**Confidence: 80** — The missing explicit integer validation means non-numeric `applicationId` values are silently treated as \"application not found\" instead of being rejected with a 400. This can make debugging confusing but is not exploitable.\n\n---\n\n## Important Issues\n\n### 7. `fetchListing` is called without `useCallback` but suppresses the deps lint warning\n\n**File:** `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/listings/[id]/ListingDetail.tsx`, lines 86, 108\n\nThe `fetchListing` function is defined without `useCallback` and the `// eslint-disable-next-line react-hooks/exhaustive-deps` comment on line 108 suppresses the warning that `fetchListing` should be in the effect's dependency array. Because `fetchListing` captures `id` from the closure, if `id` changed (e.g., if this component were reused for a different listing) the effect would not re-run with the new `fetchListing`. In the App Router with separate page instances per route this is unlikely to be triggered, but it is incorrect React practice and would cause a stale closure bug if the component were ever reused.\n\n**Confidence: 80** — The suppression comment hides a real dependency issue. The fix is to wrap `fetchListing` in `useCallback([id])` and remove the suppression comment.\n\n---\n\n### 8. `notifyGroupFull` author notification not excluded — author notified of their own group being full\n\n**File:** `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/lib/notifications.ts`, line 130\n\nThis is the same issue as item #2 above, worth restating at the code level: `listing_members` always includes the author because they are auto-joined. The loop at line 130 notifies all members including the author. There is no `WHERE user_id != author_id` filter. The author gets a \"group is now full\" notification which, combined with the separate `notifyListingAuthor` call, results in two notifications for the same event.\n\n**Confidence: 85** — Already confirmed above.\n\n---\n\n### 9. `applicationId` passed from client to PATCH body is not validated as integer\n\n**File:** `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/listings/[id]/applications/route.ts`, line 36\n\nAlready documented in item #6. The validation at line 36 is:\n\n```ts\nif (!applicationId || ![\"approve\", \"reject\"].includes(action)) {\n```\n\nThis accepts strings, floats, and objects as `applicationId`. The prepared statement will coerce these, returning no match, but the correct behavior is to explicitly validate and return 400.\n\n---\n\n## Summary\n\n| # | Severity | File | Description | Confidence |\n|---|----------|------|-------------|------------|\n| 1 | Important | `ListingDetail.tsx:86,108` | `fetchListing` not in `useCallback`, deps warning suppressed; re-fetches don't set `loading=true` | 80 |\n| 2 | Critical | `notifications.ts:130`, `join/route.ts:99-102` | Author receives duplicate notifications (new_member + group_full) for the same join event | 85 |\n| 3 | Critical | `telegram/route.ts:37`, `discord/route.ts:37` | Platform links can be set before the group is full, bypassing the intended flow and prematurely locking edit/delete | 85 |\n| 6 | Important | `applications/route.ts:36` | `applicationId` not validated as a positive integer; non-numeric values silently produce 404 instead of 400 | 80 |\n\n**Specific fixes:**\n\nFor issue #2 (`/root/Projects/bookmate/app/lib/notifications.ts` line 119), filter out the author when notifying about group full:\n```ts\nconst members = db\n  .prepare(\"SELECT user_id FROM listing_members WHERE listing_id = ? AND user_id != (SELECT author_id FROM listings WHERE id = ?)\")\n  .all(listingId, listingId) as { user_id: number }[];\n```\n\nFor issue #3 (`/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/listings/[id]/telegram/route.ts` line 20–22 and discord equivalent), add an `is_full` check after fetching the listing:\n```ts\nif (!listing.is_full) {\n  return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Group must be full before sharing a platform link\" }, { status: 400 });\n}\n```\n\nFor issue #6 (`/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/listings/[id]/applications/route.ts` line 36), change to:\n```ts\nif (!applicationId || typeof applicationId !== \"number\" || !Number.isInteger(applicationId) || applicationId < 1 || ![\"approve\", \"reject\"].includes(action)) {\n```"},{"type":"text","text":"agentId: a0d37eb2ee7074b40 (use SendMessage with to: 'a0d37eb2ee7074b40' to continue this agent)\n<usage>total_tokens: 91055\ntool_uses: 43\nduration_ms: 138986</usage>"}]}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"0f2f8ceb-12d1-4ef2-ada5-bdfef2314ecc","uuid":"8f39a636-24c9-4331-89e5-3e4ae0095b5a","timestamp":"2026-03-23T21:03:25.575Z","tool_use_result":{"status":"completed","prompt":"Review the Bookmate Next.js application for bugs, logic errors, security vulnerabilities, and code quality issues. This is round 5 of code reviews — previous rounds have already fixed:\n- Email normalization, application state handling, rate limiter leaks, link exposure\n- Validation gaps, delete notification bugs, secure cookies\n- Navbar menu issues, telegram/discord guards\n- generateMetadata crashes, wrong platform notifications\n\nFocus on areas NOT yet reviewed thoroughly:\n1. Client-side components (React hooks, state management, form handling)\n2. API route edge cases (concurrent requests, error handling paths)\n3. Database schema and query correctness\n4. Session handling edge cases\n5. Open Library API integration (error handling, XSS from external data)\n6. Notification system completeness\n\nThe app is at /root/Projects/bookmate/app/. Key directories:\n- app/app/ - App Router pages and API routes\n- app/components/ - React components\n- app/lib/ - Core libraries (db, session, notifications)\n\nReport only HIGH confidence issues that are real bugs or security problems. Include file paths and line numbers.","agentId":"a0d37eb2ee7074b40","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Now I have a thorough understanding of the codebase. If a client sends `applicationId: \"1; DROP TABLE listing_applications\"` it would be passed to the prepared statement, which is safe (SQLite parameterized queries prevent SQL injection). However, if `applicationId` is `0`, `false`, or `\"\"` the check correctly rejects it. If it is a non-integer string like `\"abc\"`, better-sqlite3 will coerce it and the `SELECT ... WHERE id = ?` will simply return no rows (404). This can make debugging confusing but is not exploitable.\n\n---\n\n## Important Issues\n\n### 7. `fetchListing` is called without `useCallback` but suppresses the deps lint warning\n\n**File:** `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/listings/[id]/ListingDetail.tsx`, lines 86, 108\n\nThe `fetchListing` function is defined without `useCallback` and the `// eslint-disable-next-line react-hooks/exhaustive-deps` comment on line 108 suppresses the warning that `fetchListing` should be in the effect's dependency array. The prepared statement will coerce these, returning no match, but the correct behavior is to explicitly validate and return 400.\n\n---\n\n## Summary\n\n| # | Severity | File | Description | Confidence |\n|---|----------|------|-------------|------------|\n| 1 | Important | `ListingDetail.tsx:86,108` | `fetchListing` not in `useCallback`, deps warning suppressed; re-fetches don't set `loading=true` | 80 |\n| 2 | Critical | `notifications.ts:130`, `join/route.ts:99-102` | Author receives duplicate notifications (new_member + group_full) for the same join event | 85 |\n| 3 | Critical | `telegram/route.ts:37`, `discord/route.ts:37` | Platform links can be set before the group is full, bypassing the intended flow and prematurely locking edit/delete | 85 |\n| 6 | Important | `applications/route.ts:36` | `applicationId` not validated as a positive integer; non-numeric values silently produce 404 instead of 400 | 80 |\n\n**Specific fixes:**\n\nFor issue #2 (`/root/Projects/bookmate/app/lib/notifications.ts` line 119), filter out the author when notifying about group full:\n```ts\nconst members = db\n  .prepare(\"SELECT user_id FROM listing_members WHERE listing_id = ? AND user_id != (SELECT author_id FROM listings WHERE id = ?)\")\n  .all(listingId, listingId) as { user_id: number }[];\n```\n\nFor issue #3 (`/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/listings/[id]/telegram/route.ts` line 20–22 and discord equivalent), add an `is_full` check after fetching the listing:\n```ts\nif (!listing.is_full) {\n  return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Group must be full before sharing a platform link\" }, { status: 400 });\n}\n```\n\nFor issue #6 (`/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/listings/[id]/applications/route.ts` line 36), change to:\n```ts\nif (!applicationId || typeof applicationId !== \"number\" || !Number.isInteger(applicationId) || applicationId < 1 || ![\"approve\", \"reject\"].includes(action)) {\n```"}],"totalDurationMs":138986,"totalTokens":91055,"totalToolUseCount":43,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":1020,"cache_read_input_tokens":87073,"output_tokens":2961,"server_tool_use":{"web_search_requests":0,"web_fetch_requests":0},"service_tier":"standard","cache_creation":{"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":1020},"inference_geo":"","iterations":[],"speed":"standard"}}}

db\n    59→    .prepare(\n    60→      \"SELECT l.author_id, l.book_title FROM listings l WHERE l.id = ?\"\n    61→    )\n    62→    .get(listingId) as { author_id: number; book_title: string } | notifyApplicationDecision(\n    95→  userId: number,\n    96→  listingId: number,\n    97→  decision: \"approved\" | \"rejected\"\n    98→) {\n    99→  const listing = db\n   100→    .prepare(\"SELECT \"application_approved\" : \"application_rejected\";\n   105→    const message =\n   106→      decision === \"approved\"\n   107→        ? `Your application to join \"${listing.book_title}\" was approved!`\n   108→        : `Your application to join \"${listing.book_title}\" was not accepted.`;\n   109→    createNotification(userId, listingId, type, message);\n   110→  }\n   111→}\n   112→\n   113→export function notifyGroupFull(listingId: number) {\n   114→  const listing = db\n   115→    .prepare(\"SELECT book_title, platform_preference FROM listings WHERE id = ?\")\n   116→ const botConfigured = listing.platform_preference === \"discord\"\n   124→      ? !!process.env.DISCORD_BOT_TOKEN\n   125→      : !!process.env.TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN;\n   126→    const message = botConfigured\n   127→      ? `The reading group for \"${listing.book_title}\" is now full! The ${platform} group will be set up automatically.`\n   128→      : `The reading group for \"${listing.book_title}\" is now full!  134→        \"group_full\",\n   135→        message\n   136→      );\n   137→    }\n   138→  }\n   139→}\n   140→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"0f2f8ceb-12d1-4ef2-ada5-bdfef2314ecc","uuid":"7c3fbd61-117a-4342-9f8d-c63b18cc79bd","timestamp":"2026-03-23T21:03:33.204Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/root/Projects/bookmate/app/lib/notifications.ts","content":"import db from \"./db\";\nimport { sendEmail } from \"./email\";\n\nfunction getUserEmail(userId: number): string | null {\n  const user = db\n    .prepare(\"SELECT email FROM users WHERE id = ?\")\n    .get(userId) as { email: string } | undefined;\n  return user?.email || null;\n}\n\nexport function createNotification(\n  userId: number,\n  listingId: number | null,\n  type: string,\n  message: string\n) {\n  db.prepare(\n    \"INSERT INTO notifications (user_id, listing_id, type, message) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?)\"\n  ).run(userId, listingId, type, message);\n\n  // Also send email notification (fire-and-forget)\n  const email = getUserEmail(userId);\n  if (email) {\n    const subject = getEmailSubject(type);\n    sendEmail(email, subject, message).catch(() => {\n      // Email sending is best-effort — don't block on failure\n    });\n  }\n}\n\nfunction getEmailSubject(type: string): string {\n  switch (type) {\n    case \"new_member\":\n      return \"Bookmate: Someone joined your reading group!\";\n    case \"group_full\":\n      return \"Bookmate: Your reading group is full!\";\n    case \"application_received\":\n      return \"Bookmate: New application for your reading group\";\n    case \"application_approved\":\n      return \"Bookmate: Your application was approved!\";\n    case \"application_rejected\":\n      return \"Bookmate: Application update\";\n    case \"rating_received\":\n      return \"Bookmate: You received a new rating\";\n    case \"listing_deleted\":\n      return \"Bookmate: A reading group has been cancelled\";\n    case \"listing_updated\":\n      return \"Bookmate: A reading group has been updated\";\n    default:\n      return \"Bookmate: Notification\";\n  }\n}\n\nexport function notifyListingAuthor(\n  listingId: number,\n  joinerName: string\n) {\n  const listing = db\n    .prepare(\n      \"SELECT l.author_id, l.book_title FROM listings l WHERE l.id = ?\"\n    )\n    .get(listingId) as { author_id: number; book_title: string } | undefined;\n\n  if (listing) {\n    createNotification(\n      listing.author_id,\n      listingId,\n      \"new_member\",\n      `${joinerName} joined your reading group for \"${listing.book_title}\"`\n    );\n  }\n}\n\nexport function notifyApplicationReceived(\n  listingId: number,\n  applicantName: string\n) {\n  const listing = db\n    .prepare(\n      \"SELECT l.author_id, l.book_title FROM listings l WHERE l.id = ?\"\n    )\n    .get(listingId) as { author_id: number; book_title: string } | undefined;\n\n  if (listing) {\n    createNotification(\n      listing.author_id,\n      listingId,\n      \"application_received\",\n      `${applicantName} applied to join your reading group for \"${listing.book_title}\"`\n    );\n  }\n}\n\nexport function notifyApplicationDecision(\n  userId: number,\n  listingId: number,\n  decision: \"approved\" | \"rejected\"\n) {\n  const listing = db\n    .prepare(\"SELECT book_title FROM listings WHERE id = ?\")\n    .get(listingId) as { book_title: string } | undefined;\n\n  if (listing) {\n    const type = decision === \"approved\" ? \"application_approved\" : \"application_rejected\";\n    const message =\n      decision === \"approved\"\n        ? `Your application to join \"${listing.book_title}\" was approved!`\n        : `Your application to join \"${listing.book_title}\" was not accepted.`;\n    createNotification(userId, listingId, type, message);\n  }\n}\n\nexport function notifyGroupFull(listingId: number) {\n  const listing = db\n    .prepare(\"SELECT book_title, platform_preference FROM listings WHERE id = ?\")\n    .get(listingId) as { book_title: string; platform_preference: string } | undefined;\n  const members = db\n    .prepare(\"SELECT user_id FROM listing_members WHERE listing_id = ?\")\n    .all(listingId) as { user_id: number }[];\n\n  if (listing) {\n    const platform = listing.platform_preference === \"discord\" ? \"Discord\" : \"Telegram\";\n    const botConfigured = listing.platform_preference === \"discord\"\n      ? !!process.env.DISCORD_BOT_TOKEN\n      : !!process.env.TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN;\n    const message = botConfigured\n      ? `The reading group for \"${listing.book_title}\" is now full! The ${platform} group will be set up automatically.`\n      : `The reading group for \"${listing.book_title}\" is now full! The organizer will share a ${platform} link soon.`;\n\n    for (const member of members) {\n      createNotification(\n        member.user_id,\n        listingId,\n        \"group_full\",\n        message\n      );\n    }\n  }\n}\n","numLines":140,"startLine":1,"totalLines":140}}}
